annotations.label: Egyptian approximation for area of a circle

anchor.snippet: approximate the area of a circle

annotations.note: The exact area πr² of a 2D ball is formalized (volume_ball_fin_two), but the specific Egyptian approximation (8d/9)² is not.

annotations.label: Volume of a frustum of a pyramid (Moscow Papyrus)

anchor.snippet: the volume of a frustum of a pyramid

annotations.note: No frustum (truncated pyramid) volume formula V=(h/3)(a²+ab+b²) is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Babylonian area of a circle

anchor.snippet: the area as one-twelfth the square of the circumference

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the exact disc area πr², but not the Babylonian approximation of area as one-twelfth the squared circumference.

annotations.label: Babylonian volume of a cylinder

anchor.snippet: The volume of a cylinder was taken as the product of the base and the height

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geometric 'volume of a cylinder = base × height' result (its 'cylinder' notions are measure-theoretic/homotopical, unrelated).

annotations.label: Babylonian volume of a frustum

anchor.snippet: the volume of the frustum of a cone or a square pyramid was incorrectly taken

annotations.note: No frustum volume formula (correct or the Babylonian incorrect one) appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Primitive Pythagorean triple (13500, 12709, 18541)

anchor.snippet: which is a primitive triple

annotations.note: The general notion and classification of primitive Pythagorean triples is formalized, of which this specific numeric triple would be an instance, but the example itself is not a declaration.

annotations.label: Three classical construction problems

anchor.snippet: three classic construction problems

annotations.note: Mathlib has no compass-and-straightedge constructibility theory (its 'constructible' decls concern constructible sets in topology/algebraic geometry), so squaring the circle/doubling the cube/angle trisection are absent.

annotations.label: Euclid's first postulate

anchor.snippet: Any two points can be joined by a straight line

annotations.note: Mathlib builds analytic, not synthetic axiomatic, geometry; the line through two points exists via affineSpan, but Euclid's postulate is not stated as an axiom.

annotations.label: Euclid's second postulate

anchor.snippet: Any finite straight line can be extended in a straight line

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Euclid's synthetic axioms; there is no 'segment extension' postulate (lines in affine space are simply unbounded by construction).

annotations.label: Euclid's third postulate

anchor.snippet: A circle can be drawn with any center and any radius

annotations.note: The Sphere structure bundles an arbitrary center and radius, realizing the postulate's content, but it is not stated as an axiom of synthetic geometry.

annotations.label: Euclid's fourth postulate

anchor.snippet: All right angles are equal to each other

annotations.note: Mathlib has no synthetic-axiom statement that all right angles are equal (right angles are simply the real number π/2, so the claim is not stated as a postulate).

annotations.label: Euclid's parallel postulate

anchor.snippet: the two lines extended will intersect

annotations.note: The parallel postulate is not formalized as an axiom/theorem in Mathlib's analytic Euclidean geometry.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's theorem (cyclic quadrilateral diagonals)

anchor.snippet: the perpendicular line drawn from the point of intersection of the diagonals

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a cyclic-quadrilateral concept (noted explicitly in the Ptolemy file's TODO), so Brahmagupta's diagonal-perpendicular theorem is absent.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's formula (area of cyclic quadrilateral)

anchor.snippet: The area, A , of a cyclic quadrilateral with sides of lengths

annotations.note: Neither Heron's formula nor its Brahmagupta generalization for cyclic quadrilateral area is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's theorem on rational triangles

anchor.snippet: A triangle with rational sides

annotations.note: No result on parameterizing triangles with rational sides, area, and diagonals appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parameshvara's circumradius of a cyclic quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the radius R of the circumscribed circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has circumradius for simplices (Simplex.circumradius) but no cyclic-quadrilateral circumradius formula like Parameshvara's.

annotations.label: Mo Jing definition of a point

anchor.snippet: definition of the geometric point

annotations.note: Points are merely elements of a space in Mathlib; the Mo Jing's notion of an indivisible point is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Mo Jing definitions of length comparison and parallels

anchor.snippet: definitions for the comparison of lengths and for parallels

annotations.note: Parallelism of affine subspaces (AffineSubspace.Parallel) and length comparison via dist are formalized, though not as the Mo Jing's definitions.

annotations.label: Mo Jing definitions of circumference, diameter, radius

anchor.snippet: definitions for circumference, diameter, and radius

annotations.note: Mathlib's Sphere has a center and radius field (and Metric.diam gives diameter), capturing radius/diameter concepts but not the Mo Jing definitions verbatim.

annotations.label: Nine Chapters geometrical area and volume formulas

anchor.section: The Nine Chapters on the Mathematical Art
anchor.snippet: listed the following geometrical formulas

annotations.note: Some individual formulas (e.g., the disc area πr²) are formalized via volume measures, but the collected list of area/volume formulas is not a single Mathlib result.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: two different general proofs of the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is formalized in both vector and angle-at-point forms (the latter as dist_sq = dist_sq + dist_sq iff the angle is π/2).

annotations.label: Analytic geometry

anchor.snippet: geometry with coordinates and equations

annotations.note: 'Analytic geometry' is an entire field rather than a single definition; Mathlib uses coordinate spaces throughout but has no decl defining the discipline.

annotations.label: Projective geometry

anchor.snippet: the study of geometry without measurement

annotations.note: Mathlib defines projective space (Projectivization) and its subspaces, but 'projective geometry' as a discipline is not a single definition.

annotations.label: Hilbert's axioms

anchor.snippet: now known as Hilbert's axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Hilbert's (or any synthetic) axiom system for geometry.

annotations.label: Regular convex polytopes in higher dimensions (Schläfli)

anchor.snippet: there are exactly six such regular convex polytopes in dimension four

annotations.note: Mathlib has no classification of regular convex polytopes (no notion of regular 4-polytope), so Schläfli's enumeration is absent.

annotations.label: Finite geometry

anchor.snippet: the study of spaces with only finitely many points

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes finite incidence structures such as projective planes (Configuration.ProjectivePlane with an order), an instance of finite geometry, but not 'finite geometry' as a general definition.
